old_claim.note: Smartphones provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: A 112-mile microwave telecommunications link connected the Nebraska facilities for live interactive care.

source_shape.source_locator: The history describes televised patient examinations and therapy, then identifies the 1964 Omaha-Norfolk microwave link as the first large-scale test of interactive television as a medical medium.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: A 112-mile microwave telecommunications link carried the Nebraska program's interactive medical television service in 1964.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The long-distance communications link directly enabled the first scoped service decades before smartphones existed.

ontology_before: Video telemedicine was modeled as requiring smartphones.
ontology_after: Video telemedicine depends on telecommunications, with smartphones retained only as a later access device.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: telemedicine_video_consults now depends on telecommunications and no longer on smartphones.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to smartphone-native telemedicine applications.
Evidence shows no telecommunications link was involved in the 1964 program.
short_reason: telecommunications directly supports the scoped system; smartphones does not.
